blurb: The first set roars with a blistering Jack Straw, fiery Lazy Lightning ‚Üí Supplication, and a bouncy Althea. The second half delivers a classic China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider jam, solid Estimated Prophet, and a rousing Sugar Magnolia finale. Overall the band is tight, with standout keyboards from Brent and crisp guitar work by Jerry.
review: On October‚ÄØ18,‚ÄØ1983 at the Cumberland County Civic Center, the Dead turned Portland into a high‚Äëoctane playground. Jerry Garcia‚Äôs guitar cut clean and fierce through Jack Straw and the soaring China Rider jam, while his tone stayed front‚Äëand‚Äëcenter on the legendary China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence. Brent Mydland‚Äôs keyboards added a friendly bounce and a subway‚Äëwheel texture that lifted the Supplication jam and gave the set a bright undercurrent. Phil Lesh‚Äôs thumping bass drove the groove, especially during the Lazy Lightning ‚Üí Supplication transition, and both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann locked in with solid pre‚Äëdrum fills and steady pulse throughout. Bob Weir fired up the crowd with a scorching slide solo on Dupree's Diamond Blues and train‚Äëhorn accents on C.C. Rider, plus strong vocal work on Black Peter and Estimated Prophet. The show‚Äôs energy is captured best by the audience tape gd83-10-18.aud.dodd.12484.sbeok.shnf, which balances clarity with the live atmosphere, making it the go‚Äëto source for this standout night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six of seven recordings are clearly positive, praising the tight playing, standout jams (Jack Straw, Lazy Lightning‚ÜíSupplication, China Rider) and strong keyboards; one mixed review still notes excellent performance. Consistent high‚Äëenergy praise across sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience tape with crystal‚Äëclear instrument detail, high energy capture, and no dropouts ‚Äì the most balanced listening experience.
